title: Xebia: Why AI agents fail without the right data foundation

raw_body:
If your remit is to help your organisation add AI agents to accelerate its processes, you have to start at the foundation – and that means making your data available for AI consumption. Agentic AI scales on data strength, as Niels Zeilemaker, global CTO at Xebia, explains. “If you don’t think about that, you can […]
The post Xebia: Why AI agents fail without the right data foundation appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: Xebia emphasizes that AI agents can only be effective if built on a solid data foundation. Niels Zeilemaker, Xebia's global CTO, highlights that the strength of agentic AI relies heavily on accessible and organized data. Without this focus, organizations risk failing to fully leverage AI's potential. This insight is particularly relevant now as more companies seek to implement AI solutions to improve efficiency.
why_it_matters: Organizations aiming to adopt AI agents must prioritize data accessibility to ensure successful implementation and performance.
This highlights a broader trend where effective AI deployment increasingly depends on robust data management practices across industries.

lenses.eli12: To make AI agents work well, companies need to have their data organized and ready for use. Think of it like building a house; if the foundation isn’t strong, everything above it could collapse. This matters because as more businesses turn to AI, having good data will help them succeed and improve their processes.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this means prioritizing data management is crucial for AI projects. Users will expect seamless interactions with AI, which requires strong data backing. Investing in data organization could lead to more efficient AI applications, ultimately enhancing user satisfaction.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the success of AI agents hinges on the quality and accessibility of the underlying data. Models like GPT-3 require structured datasets to function effectively, and without this, their performance can degrade. It's essential to ensure data integrity and organization to maximize AI capabilities.
